snapshot: The Nikkei finished the morning session at 53,658.47, down 91.15, after giving back an early rally and slipping into negative territory.
Prime-market breadth was 443 gainers, 1089 losers and 53 unchanged, showing a stock-picking tape rather than broad market strength.
USD/JPY was in the mid-159s at 11:58 JST. The market is still looking at 160, but the pair has not broken through cleanly yet.
WTI dropped into the mid-80s on ceasefire hopes and then rebounded toward the 91 area, keeping energy volatility high.
Around 11:50 JST, fresh Middle East headlines pushed U.S. stock futures lower again, cooling the morning risk-on mood into the lunch break.

drivers.driver: The early relief rally faded within the morning session
drivers.why_today: The market opened on the back of stronger U.S. equities and firmer overnight futures, but by the close of the morning session the Nikkei had slipped 91 points. Stock-specific confirmation matters more than index direction again.

drivers.driver: Middle East headlines and crude have reasserted control
drivers.why_today: Crude first broke on ceasefire hopes, then stabilized, and the 11:50 JST airstrike headline pushed U.S. futures lower again. The afternoon still carries clear geopolitical risk.

drivers.driver: Only simple, visible catalysts are still beating the tape
drivers.why_today: Capital is concentrating in names with easy-to-explain triggers such as Minato Holdings, Musashi Seimitsu, Kodama Chemical and Timee.

headline_inventory.headline: Chicago Nikkei 225 futures were 53,640, up 160 points versus Osaka
U.S. markets closed with the Dow up 305, the Nasdaq up 167, and WTI at $90.32, leaving a relief tone overnight
The Nikkei ended the morning session at 53,658.47, down 91.15, after an early rise faded into profit-taking
USD/JPY was in the mid-159s at 11:58 JST, with no convincing push through the 160 threshold yet
Israel carried out large-scale airstrikes in multiple parts of Iran, and U.S. stock futures extended their decline
Minato Holdings revised up its FY March 2026 earnings outlook
Musashi Seimitsu surged after a new 5.11% holding by GMO became visible
Timee announced a buyback capped at 1.0% of shares and 1.46 billion yen
Kodama Chemical Industry raised its FY March 2026 ordinary-profit forecast to 1.6 billion yen
INPEX and JAPEX rebounded as Iran rejected the U.S. peace proposal and WTI stayed firm

watchlist.name: Minato Holdings
watchlist.why_today: It revised up FY guidance again, and at 11:30 JST the stock was still up more than 15%, making it the cleanest positive outlier in a weak tape.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 2,500-yen area and re-attack the morning high zone
Does real dip-buying volume persist into the afternoon
Does the market keep rewarding this as a second-stage upward revision
falsifiers: A break below 2,500 yen would damage the morning bid profile
The memory-pricing tailwind is judged to be only a short-term factor
watchlist.red_team: Memory-linked names can become highly narrative-driven, which raises the risk of an afternoon pullback.

watchlist.name: Musashi Seimitsu Industry
watchlist.why_today: A new 5.11% stake by GMO became public, and the stock was still up more than 10% at 11:30 JST, showing strong flow-driven demand.
watchpoints: Can it hold around the 2,900-yen area and stay near the highs
Does turnover remain active enough for follow-through
Does the 5% filing keep the special-situation angle alive
falsifiers: The 5% filing is treated as a one-session speculation trade
The stock gives back a large part of the gain after any fresh high
watchlist.red_team: This is driven more by positioning and governance speculation than by an operating trigger, so reversals can be fast.

watchlist.name: Kodama Chemical Industry
watchlist.why_today: It raised ordinary-profit guidance from 0.85 billion yen to 1.6 billion yen, and the stock was still holding a gain in the 4% area by 11:30 JST.
watchpoints: Can it keep the 1,100-yen area and remain firm into the afternoon
Does the market keep valuing the revision as more than a one-off
Does the small-cap materials bid remain alive
falsifiers: The market reduces the move to FX-related gains only
The stock loses its morning bid and starts slipping from the high zone
watchlist.red_team: As a smaller-cap name, it can turn into a price-range trade quickly once early demand cools.

watchlist.name: Nomura Micro Science
watchlist.why_today: AI-semiconductor demand and short covering are reinforcing each other. The stock was up 9.69% at 11:30 JST after peaking at +13.5% intraday.
watchpoints: Can it hold the 3,100-yen area and keep most of the morning gain
Does short covering continue into the afternoon
Can the V-shaped FY27 narrative keep beating profit-taking
falsifiers: The move is dismissed as expectation-only buying
The short squeeze runs out of force in the afternoon
watchlist.red_team: The move is driven more by positioning and expectation than by a fresh company disclosure, so reversals can come quickly.

watchlist.name: Timee
watchlist.why_today: A buyback capped at 1.0% of shares and 1.46 billion yen is supporting the stock, which was still up 3.61% at 11:30 JST despite the softer broader tape.
watchpoints: Can it keep trading above the 1,200-yen area
Does the buyback continue to support small and mid-cap growth appetite
Can the stock absorb selling once the first buying wave slows
falsifiers: Weakness across Growth names spreads into the stock
The market judges the buyback size to be too small
watchlist.red_team: The trigger is clean, but the upside range can stay limited if the market tone worsens again.

watchlist.name: INPEX
watchlist.why_today: WTI first broke lower on ceasefire speculation and then recovered toward the 91 area, helping resource stocks preserve relative strength.
watchpoints: Can WTI stay around the 91-dollar area during Tokyo hours
Do oil and resource names keep outperforming in the afternoon
Can the stock avoid getting dragged down if broader risk-off returns
falsifiers: Crude falls sharply again and the resource bid disappears
Middle East headlines push the whole market into deeper risk-off mode
watchlist.red_team: If oil direction turns unstable again, volatility can overwhelm the fundamental support story.

deep_dive.title: Minato Holdings: the key afternoon test is whether the second upward revision still carries

lines: The core of the move is a second-stage earnings upgrade driven by stronger memory pricing.
In a weak index tape, this is exactly the kind of simple, numeric catalyst that can keep attracting short-term capital.
After the March 25 close, the company lifted its FY March 2026 outlook again, taking ordinary profit from 2.78 billion yen to 3.649 billion yen.
By 11:30 JST on March 26, the stock was at 2,536 yen, up 15.43%, confirming that the market is reacting to the revision rather than ignoring it.
Can it hold the 2,500-yen area and re-test the morning high zone in the afternoon.
Does real dip-buying volume keep showing up rather than letting the move become only an opening burst.
A drop back below 2,500 yen would damage the morning bid profile.
The market decides the memory-pricing tailwind is only a short-term factor.
Memory-linked semiconductor names can become highly narrative-driven, which raises the risk of an afternoon pullback.
If geopolitical headlines worsen and the market weakens again, the stock can still face profit-taking as a smaller-cap name.

sources.title: ミナトＨＤ(6862) 業績予想の修正に関するお知らせ

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei gives back most of the 11:00 JST gain and the market turns decisively lower again
If after-hours crude spikes sharply and turns the relief tone into a fresh energy shock
If USD/JPY suddenly jumps back toward the upper 159s or intervention-style volatility returns
If the top catalyst names such as Minato Holdings and Musashi Seimitsu fail to hold their morning high zones and start breaking down on volume
